parentQuestion: An elevated ridge of the cortex is called a ___.

scaffolds.q: The surface of the brain has a wrinkled, folded appearance.
scaffolds.explain: The cerebral cortex is magnificently wrinkled — convoluted like a crumpled bedsheet. This folding increases surface area roughly threefold. More surface area = more neurons = more processing power within the same skull volume. Physics constrains biology; biology responds with clever geometry.

scaffolds.q: A sulcus is a groove or depression on the brain surface.
scaffolds.explain: Sulci (plural) are the grooves between cortical folds. Deep sulci are called fissures. The longitudinal fissure separates hemispheres. The central sulcus divides frontal from parietal lobes. Grooves as anatomical landmarks.
